axis: bridge / alert firing event log — append-only history TSV for downstream trend analysis
title: atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa — persist FIRED outcomes to state/atlas_alert_history.tsv (append-only)
summary: Extend tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa (ω-bridge-17, ω-bridge-19) so that every __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED line also appends a structured row to state/atlas_alert_history.tsv. PASS / SKIP outcomes are deliberately not recorded — the file is an event log of firings, suitable for downstream trend analysis (alert rate over time, MTBA per rule, etc.). Schema is TAB-separated with 7 columns: ts (system clock at emit, NOT row ts) / rule / ratio / threshold / last_n / sampled / extra. Adds --no-history flag (skip append, default OFF — history written) and ATLAS_ALERT_HISTORY_TSV env override. Pre-flight seeds the canonical header on first write. Write failures emit __ATLAS_ALERT__ HISTORY_WRITE_FAIL and continue (raw 71 report-only).

predecessors.omega_bridge_17: built tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa — JSONL-stream subscriber with warn_ratio + insufficient_ratio rules; emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED/PASS/SKIP sentinels to stdout
predecessors.omega_bridge_19: added two time-shape rules (consecutive_warn streak + recent_warn_ratio window); each emits its own FIRED sentinel shape
predecessors.omega_bridge_14_force_add: established the pattern of force-adding TSV schema stubs into state/* despite the gitignore wildcard (commit 5e59259b)

context.problem: Subscriber emits FIRED sentinels to stdout but does not persist them anywhere durable. Operators can grep transcripts after the fact, but there is no structured time-series of 'when did each rule fire, with what parameters' — needed for downstream trend analysis (alert rate over time, MTBA per rule, calibration adjustment via base-rate inspection).

design_alternatives_considered.option: A — write a separate JSONL log
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: JSON parsing in shell tooling is heavier than TSV; trend analysis is naturally column-oriented (cut/awk); operators already comfortable with bridge_health_history.tsv format

design_alternatives_considered.option: B — TSV append-only inside the subscriber on FIRE only (CHOSEN)
design_alternatives_considered.chosen_because: minimal surface, mirrors bridge_health_history.tsv pattern from ω-bridge-14, append-only semantics naturally satisfy raw 77, no inter-tool coupling change (subscriber stays CLI-only)

design_alternatives_considered.option: C — wrap subscriber in a shell script that tees and parses the FIRED lines
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: duplicates information already structured inside the tool, fragile against sentinel format changes (ω-bridge-19 already added two new shapes), pushes complexity outward instead of consolidating it

edits: header @usage block: documented --no-history flag and ATLAS_ALERT_HISTORY_TSV env
header sentinels list: added __ATLAS_ALERT__ HISTORY_WRITE_FAIL shape
argv parser: --no-history flag handler (sets write_history=false); --help text extended
ATLAS_ALERT_HISTORY_TSV env resolution after argv parse (env wins over default; --no-history independent of path)

parallel event_rows array, lockstep with `fired` — each FIRED push records a TAB-separated 6-field row (rule\tratio\tthreshold\tlast_n\tsampled\textra) which the writer prepends with system-clock ts at emit time
consecutive_warn extra encoding: ratio=0.0000 threshold=0 extra='streak=K' (streak is in extra, not ratio)
recent_warn_ratio extra encoding: last_n=0 extra='window_minutes=M' (window is in extra, not last_n)

history persistence block (between human summary and sentinel emission): now_iso via `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ`; pre-flight seeds HISTORY_HEADER if file missing; per-row append_file with try/catch — failure emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ HISTORY_WRITE_FAIL and breaks out of the row loop (raw 71 report-only — does not block sentinel emission)

files_created.purpose: TSV schema stub (header-only, 0 data rows) — force-added despite state/* gitignore wildcard, mirroring bridge-14's bridge_health_history.tsv pattern
files_created.schema_header: # atlas_alert_history.tsv — alert firing event log (raw 77 append-only) / # ts<TAB>rule<TAB>ratio<TAB>threshold<TAB>last_n<TAB>sampled<TAB>extra

tsv_columns.name: ts
tsv_columns.note: system clock at emit (date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ); NOT the ts of the row that triggered the rule

tsv_columns.name: ratio
tsv_columns.note: 0.0000 for consecutive_warn (streak rule has no ratio)

tsv_columns.name: sampled
tsv_columns.note: actual rows sampled at evaluation time

new_flags: --no-history  (default OFF; when set, FIRED outcomes are not appended to history TSV)

edge_cases_handled.header_seeded_on_first_write: if history file does not exist when first FIRE occurs, seed the canonical 2-line header before appending the first row; subsequent invocations append directly
edge_cases_handled.pass_outcome_no_file_creation: PASS results in event_rows=[] so the persistence block is bypassed entirely — empty TSV not seeded if there is no firing yet
edge_cases_handled.skip_outcome_early_return: SKIP (timeline_missing or no_matching_rows) returns from preflight before reaching the persistence block — neither header seeded nor row appended
edge_cases_handled.multi_rule_firing_one_row_per_rule: event_rows tracks each fired rule independently; multiple rules in the same invocation produce multiple rows with the same ts (system clock granularity is 1s)
edge_cases_handled.no_history_flag_skips_seed_and_append: --no-history sets write_history=false; the entire persistence block is gated on write_history && len(event_rows) > 0
edge_cases_handled.env_override_wins_over_default: if ATLAS_ALERT_HISTORY_TSV is non-empty, history_path = env_history; otherwise DEFAULT_HISTORY_TSV (state/atlas_alert_history.tsv)
edge_cases_handled.write_failure_report_only: header init or append failure emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ HISTORY_WRITE_FAIL sentinel and continues — does NOT block sentinel emission of the FIRED rules themselves; ec unchanged
edge_cases_handled.append_only_never_rewrites: uses append_file (not write_file) for data rows; existing rows never modified

compliance.raw_9_hexa_only_suffix: edited an existing tracked .hexa file; the new TSV stub is the only non-.hexa file (mirrors bridge-14's force-add pattern)
compliance.raw_71_report_only: tool remains read-only on the timeline; history-write failure emits a sentinel but does not change ec
compliance.raw_73_admissibility: scope=bridge_anomaly filter unchanged; persistence is purely a side-effect of an already-decided FIRED outcome
compliance.raw_77_append_only: history TSV is append-only by construction (append_file API + only-on-FIRE gating); existing rows never modified; the timeline JSONL remains read-only
compliance.raw_80_sentinel: new __ATLAS_ALERT__ HISTORY_WRITE_FAIL shape added (path=... reason=...); existing PASS / SKIP / FIRED shapes untouched
compliance.no_parallel_session_collision: edited only tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa and force-added state/atlas_alert_history.tsv; did not touch cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own
compliance.explicit_path_commit: git add tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa + git add -f state/atlas_alert_history.tsv (no `git add -A`)
compliance.no_anima_commit: closure JSON written under /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/ but not committed to anima

fixtures.name: T1 — FIRE writes one row, header auto-seeded
fixtures.fixture: timeline_warn.jsonl (10 bridge_anomaly rows: 6 WARN + 4 PASS → warn_ratio=0.6 ≥ 0.4 fires)
fixtures.expected: 1 data row, schema-correct, with system-clock ts (not row ts)
fixtures.observed: TSV header seeded automatically; 1 row appended: 2026-04-26T12:16:27Z\twarn_ratio\t0.6000\t0.4\t10\t10\t

fixtures.name: T2 — PASS, no append, no file created
fixtures.expected: no firings → no row append → no file creation (header not seeded)
fixtures.observed: __ATLAS_ALERT__ PASS sampled=10 rules_evaluated=2; ls /tmp/bridge21_history.tsv → No such file or directory

fixtures.name: T3 — SKIP (timeline_missing), no append
fixtures.expected: preflight returns early; no row append
fixtures.observed: __ATLAS_ALERT__ SKIP reason=timeline_missing path=/tmp/nonexistent_timeline.jsonl; ls /tmp/bridge21_history.tsv → No such file or directory

fixtures.name: T4 — multi-rule firing → multiple rows (one per rule)
fixtures.fixture: timeline_multi_realtime.jsonl (6 PASS + 4 WARN with current real-system ts so window rule sees them in-window)
fixtures.expected: 3 fired sentinels, 3 data rows in TSV
fixtures.observed: warn_ratio (ratio=0.4000), consecutive_warn (streak=3 observed=4), recent_warn_ratio (ratio=0.4000 threshold=0.4 window=60 sampled=10) — all 3 rows present in TSV with same ts

fixtures.name: T5 — --no-history flag, no append even on FIRE
fixtures.expected: FIRED sentinel emits to stdout but TSV not written
fixtures.observed: __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=warn_ratio ratio=0.6000 threshold=0.4 last_n=10 sampled=10; ls /tmp/bridge21_history.tsv → No such file or directory

fixtures.name: T6 — ATLAS_ALERT_HISTORY_TSV env override redirects writes
fixtures.expected: row appended to override path; default path untouched
fixtures.observed: /tmp/bridge21_alt_history.tsv received header + 1 row; default state/atlas_alert_history.tsv stub unmodified (0 non-comment lines)

operational_notes.first_invocation_seeds_default_path: the force-added stub already has the canonical header, so first FIRE in production appends directly without a header-init step (the pre-flight is mostly relevant for fixture paths that start fresh)
operational_notes.history_growth_rate: one row per fired rule per invocation; with default cron cadence (~5min) and rules off-by-default for time-shape, expected steady-state is <1 row/day per environment unless the bridge cluster is actively degrading — long-term the file should remain manageable (<1MB/year typical)
operational_notes.back_compat: all existing invocations continue to work; --no-history is opt-in (default writes) so the new behaviour is silently active for all callers; existing FIRED sentinels on stdout unchanged

future_candidates: tool/atlas_alert_history_analyzer.hexa — read TSV, compute MTBA per rule + alert rate per day, emit __ATLAS_ALERT_TREND__ sentinels
rotation policy: when alert_history.tsv exceeds N MB, archive to state/atlas_alert_history_<yyyymm>.tsv (currently no rotation — append-only forever)
fingerprint suppression — group consecutive identical alerts by (rule, sampled) within a cooldown window, recording only the first + count
wire history TSV into a future ω-network cycle that pushes alert events into the cross-repo dashboard
